title: canistrum superbum
side_effects: Canistrum superbum can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing dizziness, nausea, and stomach cramps due to its high levels of toxic compounds. Prolonged consumption of the plant's extract can also lead to kidney damage, as it can interfere with the body's ability to filter waste. Additionally, improper use of the canistrum superbum plant has been linked to cases of allergic reactions, including skin rashes and itchy eyes. If not used correctly, the plant's toxic compounds can also exacerbate existing medical conditions, such as diabetes and high blood pressure.
